description: Calibration constants extracted from research corpus — used to parameterize TOK's sizing, regime detection, and sentiment filters.

liquidity.notes: Square-root impact law: slippage ≈ sqrt(vol / depth). Spread penalty ramps as (spread - ideal) / (threshold - ideal). Depth penalty 0.20 max for sub-$50K depth. Total penalty capped at 0.50 (half position).

regime.notes: change_24h is a decimal (0.07 = 7%). High-vol (>3%): mean-reversion papers confirm snap-back after abnormal moves. Low-vol (<1.5%): momentum papers document persistent directional flows.

sentiment.notes: Sentiment-driven price prediction papers show lagged correlation of 2-4h. Fear&Greed <20 is extreme — elevated risk regardless of signal quality.

execution.notes: Median crypto LOB spread ~5bps on liquid pairs per market microstructure papers.

portfolio.notes: Portfolio-level risk limits. concurrency_max caps open positions. drawdown_limit_pct hard-gates trading. drawdown_sizing_reduction_pct = max score reduction at full drawdown. correlation_threshold: proposed coin with r>0.7 vs existing position gets penalty. correlation_exposure_penalty: fraction of score lost for correlated adds. All thresholds from: crypto-as-asset-class papers on diversification, portfolio concentration limits, and risk budgeting.

derivatives.notes: Funding rate thresholds for BTC perpetual (8h period). Values >0.001 (% rate) signal crowded longs — squeeze risk. Values <-0.001 signal crowded shorts — short-squeeze setup. L/S ratio >1.5 = crowded long, <0.67 = crowded short. Thresholds from: derivatives pricing papers on funding rate mean-reversion and position concentration risk.
